rulings.Case Title: IN RE: J.G. WENTWORTH ORIGINATIONS LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 9, 2026, line 1. PETITIONER J.G. WENTWORTH ORIGINATIONS LLC'S Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Payment Rights. The unopposed petition for transfer of payment rights is granted based on the supplemental information that was received and the new offer that was transmitted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA CRISTINA BAEZA VARELA VS. RALPH OSTERHOUT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 9, 12026, line 14. CROSS DEFENDANT MARIA BAEZA VARELA'S DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. The unopposed demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the second amended cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend. "Before a person can be charged with a fiduciary obligation, he must either knowingly undertake to act on behalf and for the benefit of another, or must enter into a relationship which imposes that undertaking as a matter of law." (City of Hope National Medical Center v. Genentech, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 375, 386 [citation simplified].) The role of part-time bookkeeper is not a fiduciary relationship as a matter of law. The cross-complaint does not allege facts that the cross-defendant agreed to be a fiduciary; at most it discloses that the cross-complainant shared sensitive information with her. Because the court has no information about what facts, if any, cross-complainant could plead to show that there was agreement to fiduciary obligations, the court grants leave to amend. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR DIZON VS. SAN FRANCISCO METROPOLITAN TRANSIT AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 9, 2026, line 7. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. The City and County of San Francisco's unopposed motion for summary judgment is denied without prejudice. The court construes the complaint as pleading general negligence and motor vehicle causes of action against the Muni operator, Doe 1, and against the City on a vicarious liability theory. (Gov. Code, sec. 815.2, subd. (a).) The court concludes that plaintiff's claim was sufficient to place the City on notice of both of Plaintiff's theories of liability. (See Stockett v. Association of Cal. Water Agencies Joint Powers Ins. Authority (2004) 34 Cal.4th 441, 446 ["the purpose of the claim is to give the government entity notice sufficient for it to investigate and evaluate the claim"].) Exhibit B, the video of the incident on which the City relies, does not support the undisputed material facts the City offers. The version the court has reviewed depicts Muni video from 18:34:00 to 18:59:00 on September 13, 2023. According to the UMFs, the incident occurred at 7:44-7:49 p.m on September 13, a time not depicted in Exhibit B. Thus, the City has failed in its burden as the moving party to present evidence that shifts the burden to plaintiff of showing a triable issue. (See Thatcher v. Lucky Stores, Inc. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 1081, 1086 ["While subdivision (b) of section 437c allows the court, in its discretion, to grant summary judgment if the opposing party fails to file a proper separate statement, this provision does not authorize doing so without first determining that the moving party has met its initial burden of proof."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Reconsideration Of Minute Order Granted Defeant'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof, Plaintiff'S Declaration
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 9, 2026, line 10. PLAINTIFF ARTSIOM KHATKEVICH'S Motion Of Reconsideration Of Minute Order Granted Defendant's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion for reconsideration is denied. A motion for reconsideration must rely on "new or different facts, circumstances, or law." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1008, subd. (a).) A motion for reconsideration does not lie where it is based on simple disagreement with a prior order. Plaintiff's motion provides no facts, circumstances, or law that could not have been presented in opposition to the motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG VS. FIFTH THIRD BANK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 9, 2026, line 12. HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. Continued for hearing to January 22, 2026 per Plaintiff's December 27, 2025 request. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
